projects.title: Robot Hand Real-Time Teleoperation Interface
projects.shortSubtitle: Browser-based Hand Tracking for Robotic Hand Control
projects.summary: A real-time 3D visualization platform that uses webcam-based hand tracking to control robotic hand models entirely in the browser. Built with MediaPipe and Three.js, it tracks 21 hand keypoints via AI and maps them to 31 different robotic hand models with zero installation required.
projects.content:
Real-Time 3D Robot Hand Control

A browser-based platform that uses webcam hand tracking to control robotic hand models in real time. No installation required — everything runs directly in the browser using MediaPipe for AI-powered hand keypoint detection and Three.js for 3D rendering.

Key Features

  • 21-point hand tracking via MediaPipe Hands AI model
  • 31 robotic hand models supported through URDF parsing
  • Zero installation — runs entirely in the browser
  • Real-time visualization with React Three Fiber and Three.js
  • State management via Zustand for responsive UI

How It Works

  1. Webcam captures hand movements via MediaPipe
  2. AI model extracts 21 keypoints per hand in real time
  3. Keypoints are mapped to joint angles on the selected robotic hand model
  4. Three.js renders the robotic hand responding to your movements

projects.title: Snapchat Glass Bridge Challenge
projects.shortSubtitle: Turn-Based Multiplayer Glass Bridge Game
projects.summary: A Squid Game-inspired endless runner built as a full async multiplayer game in Lens Studio. Features a custom game manager system for turn-based competition via Snapchat messages.
projects.content:
Snapchat Glass Bridge Challenge (2025)

Bridge Challenge combines Squid Game's glass bridge with endless runner mechanics. Built as a full async multiplayer game in Lens Studio. My programming work set up game manager system based on the newly released Turn Based Game feature in Lens Studio.

Tech Stack:
  • Platform: Lens Studio (Snapchat AR)
  • Language: TypeScript (ES2019)
  • Architecture: Event-driven + Handler Pattern
  • Character: Bitmoji SDK Integration
  • Multiplayer: Async via Snapchat Messages

Core Challenges

  • Early async multiplayer games after Lens Studio released their Turn Based Game feature.
  • Async data handling through Snapchat message

Core Concepts

  • Inspired by Subway Surfer + Squid Game
  • Turn-based competition via messages
  • Cute 3D fantasy world with Bitmoji
  • 2–3 minute gameplay sessions
  • Win by using fewer attempts

How the game works for 2 players

  1. Player 1 attempts the bridge challenge
  2. Game tracks attempts until success
  3. Share via Snapchat message
  4. Player 2 faces same challenge
  5. Winner = fewer attempts

System Design for Scalable Game Complexity and Use for Future Games

TurnBasedGameManager/
├── TurnBasedHandler.ts   # Turn logic
├── LevelHandler.ts       # Platform gen
├── UIHandler.ts          # UI states
└── TurnBasedBlock.ts     # Collisions

The Handler Pattern

The system employs a modular handler pattern where each handler owns a specific domain of game logic. This architecture separates concerns cleanly—TurnBasedHandler manages multiplayer turn logic without knowing how platforms generate, while LevelHandler creates levels without caring about UI states. Each handler communicates through GameManager main script and events, not direct references.

Event-Driven Communication

Besides tightly coupled components, the system also uses an event bus for all inter-handler communication and game stage transitions. Setting up registration on the start and end of each stage enables future creators to use this system for other games and enables scaling up more complex mechanics, such as transition animations.

Prototype Progress Recordings

  • Early prototype of the level in Lens Studio with generated endless platforms.
  • Each row has 2 blocks, with one that's holding you and one that's fake.

2025-08-29

  • New game models added to the game
  • UI added to show more information about the game
  • Relay information through Snapchat works for 2 players with data transferring successfully, affecting game UI display

2025-09-03

An endless version, where each player unlocks new levels with more rows when they reach the end.

Cooperative idea: the second player tries based on what the first player did, which makes it infinite levels.

Competitive idea: Compete with fewer tries for completing a level or the furthest level reached.

projects.title: GoGreenNext - Modular Map Tiles
projects.shortSubtitle: 3D Modeling for NYU Game Project
projects.summary: Created modular 3D map tiles for GoGreenNext, an NYU game project. Designed reusable tile assets in Blender to enable flexible level design and efficient world building. The modular system allows for quick assembly of diverse game environments while maintaining visual consistency.
projects.content:
GoGreenNext - Modular Map Tiles (2024)


GoGreenNext is an NYU game project where I created a modular 3D tile system for efficient level design. Built reusable map assets in Blender that enable flexible world construction while maintaining visual consistency.

Tech Stack:
  • 3D Software: Blender
  • Project Type: Game Asset Creation
  • Design System: Modular Tile Architecture
  • Collaboration: NYU Game Dev Team
  • Features: Reusable tiles, Grid-based system

Design Goals

  • Create modular tiles that seamlessly connect
  • Enable rapid level design iteration
  • Maintain visual consistency across maps
  • Optimize for game engine performance

Tile System Features

  • Grid-Based Design: Standardized dimensions for easy assembly
  • Seamless Connections: Edge-matched geometry for smooth transitions
  • Variety Pack: Multiple tile types (straight, corner, junction, etc.)
  • Optimized Geometry: Low-poly models for game performance

Workflow

  1. Design tile template with standardized grid
  2. Model base geometry in Blender
  3. Create variations (terrain, paths, structures)
  4. Test connections and edge matching
  5. Export for game engine integration

Modular Tile System

Tile Categories

  • Base Tiles: Ground, grass, dirt paths
  • Corner Pieces: 90 degrees turns and transitions
  • Junction Tiles: T-intersections, crossroads
  • Edge Tiles: Borders and boundaries
  • Feature Tiles: Trees, rocks, structures

Design Principles

  • Consistent scale and proportions
  • Snappable grid alignment
  • UV mapping for texture flexibility
  • LOD-ready geometry

Gallery

projects.title: CuraLoop: AI Companion for Alzheimer's Care
projects.shortSubtitle: 1-day Humans & AI Hackathon project in Seattle
projects.summary: Through AI Chat + Game, the app monitors cognitive and game behavior to analyze and monitor the level of Alzheimer's care needed for anyone.
projects.content:
CuraLoop: AI Companion for Alzheimer's Care (2025)

🏥 1-day Humans & AI Hackathon project in Seattle

Monitors cognitive health through AI conversations and gamified exercises to predict Alzheimer's care needs. Built with a Human-in-the-Loop model connecting patients, caregivers, and doctors for continuous, empathy-driven care.

Tech Stack (Built in 6 hours):
  • Frontend: React Native (TypeScript) via Expo
  • Backend: FastAPI (Python)
  • AI/ML: Claude Sonnet Model, Custom trend analysis
  • Deployment: Vercel
  • Team: 3-person collaborative development

Links:
  • 🚀 Live Demo
  • 💻 GitHub Repository

The Problem

Alzheimer's symptoms often progress silently between infrequent clinical visits. Current care models rely on subjective, fragmented assessments, which means cognitive decline often goes unnoticed until it becomes severe.

This leads to:
  • Delayed interventions
  • Missed opportunities for improving quality of life
  • Increased strain on caregivers
  • Lack of continuous monitoring data for doctors

The Solution

CuraLoop introduces an AI-powered daily companion that continuously tracks, predicts, and supports a patient's cognitive and emotional well-being.

Through daily check-ins, gamified cognitive exercises, and natural language conversations, the platform captures subtle behavioral, linguistic, and emotional signals.

Our AI algorithms analyze these trends to detect potential deterioration, issuing alerts to doctors or caregivers to enable proactive and early intervention.  

Core Features

🏠 Home Screen

The main interface where patients start their day. Features a clean, senior-friendly design with large buttons and clear navigation to daily check-ins, games, and chat functions.

Key Elements:
Through Chatbot:
  • Quick access to daily check-in
  • 24/7 chat companion entry
  • Progress tracking display
  • Upcoming reminders and notifications

Through Game:
  • Game library navigation
  • Game behavior recording
  • Cognitive game as brain exercise


💬 24/7 AI Chat Companion

An empathetic AI assistant powered by Claude Sonnet that patients can converse with at any time. The system analyzes language patterns, emotional tone, and conversation coherence to identify confusion, depression, or behavioral changes.

Features:
  • Natural language conversations
  • Emotional sentiment analysis
  • Memory recall prompts
  • Confusion detection and gentle redirection
  • Real-time empathetic responses
  • Conversation history logging for doctors

🎮 Gamified Cognitive Training

AI-adaptive games designed to enhance memory, attention, and orientation while generating quantitative performance data. Games adjust difficulty based on patient performance to maintain engagement without frustration.

Game Types:
  • Memory matching exercises
  • Pattern recognition challenges
  • Word recall games
  • Visual-spatial puzzles
  • Attention training activities

Each game session generates performance metrics that feed into the AI analysis system for trend detection.  

🩺 Doctor Dashboard

A comprehensive web dashboard for healthcare providers that displays patient cognitive trajectories, emotional trends, and detailed activity logs. Doctors can review AI-generated insights and make informed care decisions.

Dashboard Features:
  • Patient overview with key metrics
  • Cognitive performance trend charts
  • Emotional health indicators
  • Alert review and approval system
  • Conversation transcript analysis
  • Treatment plan modification tools
  • Export reports for clinical records

Additional Features

🗓️ Daily Check-Ins

Establishes a consistent cognitive baseline through simple memory and orientation questions. Patients answer questions about the date, recent events, and personal information to track day-to-day cognitive stability.

🔔 Predictive Alert System

AI models continuously analyze user data to detect negative trends such as declining memory performance, increased confusion, or emotional distress. When concerning patterns are identified, the system automatically triggers notifications to designated caregivers or doctors for immediate attention.  

⚕️ Human-in-the-Loop Review

Doctors receive auto-generated risk summaries with AI-detected patterns. They can review findings, approve or modify assessments, and update treatment plans. This ensures AI insights are validated by medical professionals before any care adjustments are made.

📊 Continuous Monitoring

All patient interactions-from games to conversations-are logged and analyzed to create a comprehensive picture of cognitive health over time. This longitudinal data enables more accurate predictions and personalized care strategies.  

AI + Human-in-the-Loop Workflow

Our system creates a continuous feedback loop that ensures both precision and safety:

  1. User Interaction - Patients engage with daily check-ins, games, and conversations
  2. Data Logging - All interactions are securely captured and timestamped
  3. AI Analysis & Pattern Detection - Claude Sonnet and custom ML models identify trends
  4. Risk Alert Generation - System automatically flags concerning patterns
  5. Doctor/Caregiver Review - Human experts review AI findings and context
  6. Treatment Plan Adjustment - Approved changes are implemented
  7. Continuous Feedback Loop - Updates inform future AI predictions

This process leverages AI for real-time monitoring and prediction, while human experts provide the critical oversight needed for safety, interpretability, and ethical decision-making.  

System Architecture

Mobile Application (React Native)

Frontend/
├── Daily Check-in Module
├── Game Engine
├── Chat Interface
├── Alert System
└── Offline Sync Handler
  • Built with Expo for cross-platform deployment
  • TypeScript for type safety
  • Offline-first architecture with sync
  • Real-time chat with Claude Sonnet API

Backend API (FastAPI)

Backend/
├── User Management
├── Data Analytics Engine
├── ML Trend Analysis
├── Alert Trigger System
└── Doctor Dashboard API
  • RESTful API with FastAPI
  • Python-based ML models for trend detection
  • Integration with Claude Sonnet for conversations
  • Automated alert generation

24-Hour Development Sprint

Technical Challenges & Solutions for building the app in 6 hours

Challenge 1: Real-time AI Analysis
Problem: Need to analyze conversations in real-time without lag
Solution: Implemented streaming responses from Claude Sonnet API with client-side caching

Challenge 2: Trend Detection
Problem: Limited time to build complex ML models
Solution: Used statistical analysis with rolling averages and anomaly detection

Challenge 3: Human-in-the-Loop Balance
Problem: Balancing automation with human oversight
Solution: Created tiered alert system-minor changes logged, major changes require doctor approval

projects.title: B612 Soccer
projects.shortSubtitle: Ranked in the Top 2% out of 3,300+ entries in GMTK Game Jam 2025
projects.summary: A super fun local 2-player soccer game set on a tiny looping planet. Compete against a friend where shots can loop the entire world and lead to unexpected goals! Created as a solo developer for the GMTK Game Jam 2025 ('Loop' theme). Minimalist pencil-sketched art, unique circular physics, and quick chaotic rounds.
projects.content:
The Development Challenge

B612 Soccer was created as a solo developer entry for GMTK Game Jam 2025, one of the largest game jams in the world. The theme was "Loop" — and the interpretation was literal: a soccer game on a tiny, looping planet inspired by The Little Prince's asteroid B-612.

GMTK 2025 Results

Category Rank
OverallTop 2% out of 3,300+ entries
FunTop rated
CreativityTop rated

Theme Interpretation

The planet is small enough that a kicked ball loops around the entire world. Shots wrap around the sphere, leading to unexpected ricochets and goals from behind. This creates a unique twist on classic soccer mechanics.

Core Mechanics

  • Looping planet — the ball wraps around the tiny spherical world
  • Local 2-player — compete head-to-head on the same screen
  • Quick chaotic rounds — fast-paced matches with unexpected physics
  • Minimalist controls — easy to pick up, hard to master

Art & Audio Production

All art was hand-drawn in a pencil-sketch style, giving the game a charming storybook aesthetic. Audio was composed and mixed during the jam period. The entire game — code, art, audio, and design — was produced solo within the 48-hour time limit.

Solo Process

As a one-person team, every aspect of development was handled end-to-end: game design, Unity programming, 2D art creation in Photoshop, audio production, and playtesting. The constraint of working solo pushed creative problem-solving and rapid iteration.

projects.title: Hardware Store Smart Search
projects.shortSubtitle: Lead generation tool for hardware manufacturers
projects.summary: When I worked for a hardware manufacturer, we were looking for a better way to find targeted customers (hardware stores) to purchase our stock. We built a smart search tool to crawl Google Maps business data and help identify store leads around the world.
projects.content:
Google Maps Crawling Tool for Hardware Manufacturers

When working for a hardware manufacturer, the team needed a better way to find targeted customers — hardware stores — to purchase stock. This tool crawls Google Maps business data and helps identify store leads around the world.

Core Features

  • Location-based search — search for hardware stores by city, region, or country
  • Deduplication — automatically removes duplicate business listings
  • Caching — stores previous search results for faster repeat queries
  • AI Analysis via Perplexity API — enriches business data with intelligent insights
  • Cold email generation — drafts outreach emails based on store profile data

How It Works

  1. Enter a location and search query (e.g., "hardware stores in Berlin")
  2. The tool crawls Google Maps for matching businesses
  3. Results are deduplicated and cached locally
  4. Perplexity API analyzes each result for relevance and contact info
  5. Generate cold outreach emails tailored to each store

Version History

  • v1.0 — Basic search and results display
  • v3.0 — Full pipeline with AI analysis, deduplication, caching, and email generation

projects.title: AR Drawing Tool
projects.shortSubtitle: Procedural mesh generation with hand tracking
projects.summary: During my internship at Snapchat in 2022, I had this crazy idea nobody had done before. I integrated procedural mesh generation with hand tracking, enabling users to draw dynamically in 3D space.
projects.content:
Snap Inc. Internship Project (2022)

During my internship at Snap Inc., I developed a novel AR drawing tool that integrates procedural mesh generation with hand tracking, enabling users to draw dynamically in 3D space using their hands. This was a first-of-its-kind feature in Lens Studio.

Technical Innovation

  • Procedural mesh generation — real-time geometry creation based on hand movement trajectories
  • Hand tracking integration — uses Lens Studio's hand tracking API to capture finger positions
  • 3D spatial drawing — users draw in full 3D space, not on a flat plane

Impact

  • Published official Lens Studio assets — reduced setup time by 80% for other developers
  • 200K+ plays in the first month after publication
  • Authored official documentation for the procedural mesh feature on Snap's developer portal

Recognition

The work was recognized internally and externally as a creative application of hand tracking technology. The published assets and documentation continue to help AR developers build similar experiences on the Snapchat platform.

projects.title: Ping Pong Game
projects.shortSubtitle: 2D game with 3D illusion built from scratch
projects.summary: A complete 2D game that gives 3D illusion. An interactive automate enemy that you play against. Game Engine built from scratch using p5js to manage game asset, sound, levels and game controls.
projects.content:
A Personal Connection

This project is deeply personal. I've played ping pong for over 20 years, competed in professional leagues in China, and won a championship at Columbia University. Building a digital version of the sport I love was both a technical challenge and a labor of love.

Technical Highlights

  • 3D-to-2D projection without WebGL — created a convincing 3D perspective effect using only 2D canvas operations in p5.js
  • Custom capsule collider — implemented precise collision detection for the ball and paddle interaction
  • Unity-style scene manager — built a custom scene management system in p5.js, inspired by Unity's architecture, to handle menus, gameplay, and transitions

Game Features

  • Realistic 3D perspective on a 2D canvas
  • Automated AI opponent with adjustable difficulty
  • Sound effects and game state management
  • Multiple levels and gameplay modes
  • Custom game engine built from scratch in p5.js

mediaGallery.alt: Ping Pong Game - Playable in browser
mediaGallery.title: Ping Pong Game

projects.title: No Job Too Small
projects.shortSubtitle: Ranked in the Top 1% out of 3,300+ entries in GMTK Game Jam 2024
projects.summary: Step into the absurdly large shoes of an oversized intern in a tiny 90s-style office. In this hilarious, physics-based game, your exaggerated scale turns mundane office tasks into chaotic challenges. Smash your way through the day or tiptoe to success—the choice is yours. No Job Too Small flips the theme 'Build to Scale' on its head.
projects.content:
GMTK Game Jam 2024 — Top 1%

No Job Too Small was created for the GMTK Game Jam 2024, one of the world's largest game jams. The theme was "Build to Scale", which we flipped on its head: instead of building things to scale, you ARE the scale problem.

The Concept

You play as an oversized intern in a tiny 90s-style office. Your exaggerated scale turns mundane office tasks into chaotic, physics-based challenges. Smash your way through the day or tiptoe to success — the choice is yours.

Game Features

  • Physics-based chaos — your massive size interacts with every object in the office
  • Scale-based puzzles — tasks designed around your absurd proportions
  • 90s office aesthetic — retro visual style with nostalgic charm
  • Multiple approaches — brute force or delicate precision, both are valid strategies

Team & Credits

Lingyi Zhou served as Lead Coder, responsible for core gameplay programming, physics systems, and game architecture. The game was developed collaboratively under the LookAway Games team name.

mediaGallery.alt: No Job Too Small - GMTK 2024 Gameplay

projects.title: Drag Task In - Personal Schedule Assistant
projects.shortSubtitle: Drag-and-drop schedule builder
projects.summary: A drag-and-drop schedule builder that supports offline editing and syncs automatically across devices. Designed for productivity addicts who want control over time and print-ready exports.
projects.content:
Built in Under 4 Hours

Drag Task In is a personal schedule assistant built with speed and practicality in mind. The entire first version was designed and developed in under 4 hours, proving that useful productivity tools don't need weeks of development.

Core Features

  • Drag-and-drop scheduling — intuitive task management with @dnd-kit
  • Real-time sync — changes sync automatically across devices via Supabase
  • Offline support — works without internet and syncs when back online
  • Print-ready exports — generate clean layouts for physical planning

Design Philosophy

The goal was to create a tool that feels as natural as moving sticky notes on a desk. No complex menus, no configuration screens — just drag tasks where they belong and get on with your day.

mediaGallery.alt: Drag Task In - Personal Schedule Assistant - Image 2

projects.title: Marvel's Flerken AR Filter
projects.shortSubtitle: Official AR Marketing Campaign for The Marvels Movie
projects.summary: An interactive AR filter that transforms cats into Flerkens for The Marvels movie campaign. Features real-time object detection and cross-platform deployment on TikTok and Regal Cinema app, achieving viral distribution and significant user engagement.
projects.content:
Commercial AR Campaign for The Marvels (2023)

Developed as an official AR marketing campaign for The Marvels movie during my time at Moviebill/Really AR. The filter transforms real cats into Flerkens — the tentacle-sprouting alien creatures from the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

Technical Features

  • Cat-to-Flerken transformation — real-time AR overlay that detects cats and adds Flerken visual effects
  • Real-time object detection — ML-based cat detection for accurate tracking
  • Cross-platform deployment — published on both TikTok (via Effect House) and the Regal Cinema app (via Lens Studio)

Impact

The filter was part of an official studio marketing push for The Marvels theatrical release. Deployed across multiple platforms to maximize reach, combining entertainment with brand engagement for the Marvel franchise.

projects.title: No-Drill Curtain Hook
projects.shortSubtitle: Renter-friendly 3D printed curtain solution
projects.summary: A 3D modeling and printing project to create customized hooks for home hardware. Iterated through several prototypes using Fusion 360 and tested for real-world use.
projects.content:
A Renter-Friendly Solution

Designed for renters who can't drill holes in their walls. This two-part hook system clips into the ceiling-wall seam, providing a sturdy curtain hanging point without any permanent modifications.

Design & Fabrication

  • 3D modeling in Fusion 360 with precise measurements for standard ceiling-wall gaps
  • 3D printed in PLA — durable, lightweight, and easy to reproduce
  • Two-part design — one piece grips the seam, the other provides the hook
  • Iterated through several prototypes to optimize grip strength and ease of installation

Installation

  1. Identify the ceiling-wall seam where the curtain rod needs to hang
  2. Slide the grip piece into the seam
  3. Attach the hook piece to the grip
  4. Hang curtain rod or fabric

Results

The hook supports standard curtain weight without any wall damage. A practical example of how 3D printing can solve everyday problems for apartment dwellers.

projects.title: Just Another Day
projects.shortSubtitle: Narrative-driven game with emotional arc
projects.summary: A Unity narrative-driven game project composed of five mini-games tied together through an emotional storyline. Designed to create an emotional arc that ends in catharsis.
projects.content:
A Narrative Indie Game

Just Another Day is a narrative-driven indie game composed of 5 symbolic mini-games that together tell an emotional story. Each mini-game represents a different emotional state, connected through a procedural stitching mechanic that weaves the narrative together.

Game Design

  • 5 symbolic mini-games — each represents a stage in the emotional arc
  • Procedural stitching mechanic — a recurring gameplay element that ties the narrative together
  • Custom HLSL shaders — visual effects that reflect the emotional tone of each segment
  • Emotional arc design — structured to build toward catharsis

Technical Highlights

  • Built in Unity with C#
  • Custom shader development in HLSL for mood-driven visual effects
  • Scene transitions designed to maintain emotional continuity
  • 3D assets modeled in Blender

projects.title: VR Experience on Oculus Quest
projects.shortSubtitle: Immersive object interaction game
projects.summary: An immersive game for Oculus Quest focusing on object interaction and spatial memory. Built using Unity's XR framework and tested across headset configurations.
projects.content:
Spatial Storytelling in VR

An immersive VR experience built for the Oculus Quest that explores spatial storytelling through room-scale navigation and interactive objects. Players move through environments, discover narrative elements, and interact with the world using natural hand and controller inputs.

Core Features

  • Room-scale navigation — physical movement mapped to virtual space
  • Object interaction — pick up, examine, and use objects to progress
  • Dual locomotion — supports both teleportation and smooth locomotion for different comfort levels
  • Spatial storytelling — narrative conveyed through environment design and object placement

Technical Details

  • Built with Unity's XR framework for Oculus Quest
  • Optimized for standalone VR performance
  • Custom interaction system for natural object handling
  • Tested across multiple headset configurations

projects.title: Creative Coding Experiments
projects.shortSubtitle: Generative art sketches using p5.js
projects.summary: This is a collection of my creative coding sketches built with p5.js. Each piece explores different generative or interactive ideas—from visual patterns to playful simulations. Experiments include procedural pattern generators, randomized grid-based art, and color palettes.
projects.content:
Generative Art with p5.js

A collection of creative coding sketches exploring procedural generation, interactive visuals, and algorithmic art. Each piece is an experiment in turning code into visual expression.

Sketch Categories

  • Procedural patterns — algorithmically generated visual patterns with mathematical foundations
  • Randomized grid art — grid-based compositions with controlled randomness for variety
  • Color palettes & rotation animations — explorations of color theory and motion through code

Tools & Approach

All sketches are built with p5.js in JavaScript. The focus is on rapid experimentation — each sketch is a self-contained exploration of a single visual idea, from concept to finished piece in a short session.

projects.title: 3D Poster Design with Landmarks
projects.shortSubtitle: Visualizing cities with 3D landmarks
projects.summary: This poster series visualizes cities from around the world using 3D landmarks generated in Blender. Real-world data from Google Maps was imported in both realistic and stylized formats, each lit based on the local time of day using HDRIs and custom lighting.
projects.content:
City Landmarks in 3D

A poster series that visualizes cities from around the world using 3D-rendered landmarks. Real-world geographic data from Google Maps API was imported into Blender and rendered in two distinct styles.

Design Approach

  • Google Maps API data import — real building footprints and landmark positions brought into Blender
  • Dual rendering styles — each city rendered in both realistic and stylized versions
  • HDRI lighting — environment lighting matched to local time of day for each city
  • Custom lighting setups — supplementary lights to highlight architectural details

Workflow

  1. Extract city landmark data from Google Maps API
  2. Import geographic data into Blender
  3. Model and texture key landmarks
  4. Set up HDRI lighting based on local time
  5. Render in both realistic and stylized passes
  6. Compose final poster layouts

projects.title: 3D Modeled Teddy Bear
projects.shortSubtitle: Beginner sculpting exercise in Blender
projects.summary: A soft toy modeled entirely in Blender as a beginner's sculpting exercise. This teddy bear project explored mesh shaping, subdivision modifiers, and basic material texturing in a fun and personal 3D workflow.
projects.content:
Blender Sculpting Exercise

A soft teddy bear modeled entirely in Blender as a sculpting exercise. This project focused on developing organic modeling skills through a fun, approachable subject.

Techniques Used

  • Subdivision surfaces — smooth mesh topology for soft, rounded forms
  • Basic fabric shader — material setup to simulate plush textile appearance
  • Sculpting workflow — shaping the base mesh with Blender's sculpt tools
  • Mesh optimization — clean topology for smooth rendering

projects.title: No Job Too Small – GMTK Game Jam 2024
projects.shortSubtitle: Creative game development under tight constraints
projects.summary: A creative game development project completed for the GMTK Game Jam. Demonstrates rapid prototyping skills, innovative game mechanics, and the ability to deliver compelling experiences under extreme time constraints.
projects.content:
06 No Job Too Small – GMTK Game Jam 2024

A creative game development project completed for the GMTK Game Jam. Demonstrates rapid prototyping skills, innovative game mechanics, and the ability to deliver compelling experiences under extreme time constraints.

Key Features

  • React — Core technology powering this project
  • Unity — Core technology powering this project
  • Lens Studio — Core technology powering this project
  • Three.Js — Core technology powering this project

Technical Implementation

Built using a modern technology stack:

  • React — Key component in the project architecture
  • Unity — Key component in the project architecture
  • Lens Studio — Key component in the project architecture
  • Three.Js — Key component in the project architecture
  • Typescript — Key component in the project architecture
  • Python — Key component in the project architecture

  • Blender — Key component in the project architecture

projects.content:
Dragon Roaster (Early 2021)

A captivating fantasy game where players train and battle dragons in strategic combat. Features rich storytelling, beautiful 3D environments, and complex gameplay mechanics that challenge both tactical thinking and reflexes.

Key Features

  • Dragon Training — Raise and customize your dragon companions
  • Strategic Combat — Turn-based battles with tactical depth
  • Rich Story — Epic fantasy narrative with meaningful choices
  • 3D Environments — Beautifully rendered fantasy worlds

projects.title: VR Chess (2021)
projects.shortSubtitle: Virtual reality chess with immersive 3D gameplay
projects.summary: A revolutionary virtual reality chess experience that brings the classic game to life in immersive 3D space. Players can walk around the board, pick up pieces with their hands, and experience chess like never before with spatial audio and haptic feedback.
projects.content:
VR Chess (2021)

A revolutionary virtual reality chess experience that brings the classic game to life in immersive 3D space. Players can walk around the board, pick up pieces with their hands, and experience chess like never before with spatial audio and haptic feedback.

Key Features

  • 3D Board — Physically walk around and interact with the chess board
  • Hand Controls — Natural piece manipulation with VR controllers
  • Spatial Audio — Hear pieces move in 3D space
  • Multiple Views — Change perspective for optimal gameplay

Technical Implementation

Built using cutting-edge development technologies:

  • React — Essential component in the project architecture
  • Unity — Essential component in the project architecture

projects.title: 3D Snake Game (2019)
projects.shortSubtitle: Classic snake game reimagined in 3D space
projects.summary: A modern reinterpretation of the classic snake game, transformed into a 3D space adventure. Features smooth controls, dynamic obstacles, power-ups, and progressively challenging levels that test both reflexes and spatial awareness.
projects.content:
3D Snake Game (2019)

A modern reinterpretation of the classic snake game, transformed into a 3D space adventure. Features smooth controls, dynamic obstacles, power-ups, and progressively challenging levels that test both reflexes and spatial awareness.

Key Features

  • 3D Movement — Snake moves through three-dimensional space
  • Dynamic Obstacles — Challenging barriers and power-ups
  • Progressive Difficulty — Levels increase in complexity
  • Visual Effects — Smooth animations and particle effects
